advantages: A dedicated scale branch in the FE pipeline lets distance- and penalty-sensitive models use engineered ratios and logs fairly instead of letting the widest column dominate.
ColumnTransformer wiring scales only continuous numerics, keeping one-hot columns interpretable and avoiding unnecessary transforms on binary indicators.
Skipping the scaler for tree-only pipelines reduces serialized artifacts and iteration time when algorithm sensitivity does not require rescaling.
Bundling the scaler inside the same Pipeline as the estimator enforces train-only fit discipline across feature-engineering experiments.
disadvantages: Adding a scaler branch to every experiment—even for tree models—slows iteration and confuses reviewers when scaling does not change tree behavior.
Scaling before the engineered schema stabilizes forces repeated refits whenever columns are added or dropped, increasing pipeline churn.
Branch-level mistakes (scaling one-hot columns or forgetting to scale a high-magnitude engineered ratio) are easy to miss in wide ColumnTransformer configs.
Serving pipelines that omit the fitted scaler while the training notebook scaled manually create silent train–serve skew on engineered features.
